original: John IV begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Pope Clement II, [Suitger] elected
Cardinal Benedetto Gaetani chosen as Pope Boniface VIII
400 Burgundian soldiers freeze to death during siege of Nancy
Compromise of the Nobles in Habsburg Netherlands closes against the Inquisition
Morisco Revolt against Spanish suppression in the mountainous Alpujarra region of Granada, begins with a wave of violence led by Aben Humeya
Storm hits the North Holland port of Texel: 40 ships are lost and about 500 seamen are killed
Jan van Riebeeck departs for Cape of Good Hope to found 1st permanent European settlement
Swedish troops occupy Norway
Royal Alcázar of Madrid burns down in Madrid with the lost of many artworks, though paintings of Velázquez are saved
Russia & Britain sign Second anti-French Coalition
Karol Kurpiński's melodramatic opera "The Reward, or the Revival of the Polish Kingdom" premieres at the Warsaw Opera
Christmas carol "Silent Night" composed by Franz Xaver Gruber is first sung at St Nicholas parish church in Oberndorf, Austria

Chi Phi Society forms at the College of New Jersey in Princeton, said to be the first US fraternity; disbands after a year, but later revives and merges with similar groups across the nation as part of the Chi Pi Fraternity
1st US Negro hospital founded by whites chartered, Savannah, Georgia
HMS Beagle anchors in Wigwam Bay at Cape Receiver
First great fire of early San Francisco starts east side of the Plaza, first of great seven fires in 2 years
Fire devastates US Library of Congress in Washington, destroys 35,000 volumes
William Henry Fry's "Santa Claus: Christmas Symphony" premiere performance by conductor Louis Antoine Jullien's orchestra at  Metropolitan Hall, NYC
Actor Joseph Jefferson's dramatization of "Rip Van Winkle" premieres in NYC
Battle of Gordonsville, Virginia
Several Confederate veterans form Ku Klux Klan in Pulaski, Tennessee
Giusseppi Verdi's "Aida" opera premieres in Cairo, Egypt
Pope Pius IX proclaims a jubilee for 1875
Austria-Hungary officially recognizes KingLeopold II's Congo Free State
Daniel Stover and William Hance patent a bicycle with a back-pedal brake
Scheveningse fishing boats destroyed by storm
George Vanderbilt opens Biltmore estate, the largest privately owned house in America at 178,926 square feet (16,622.8 m2) in Asheville, North Carolina
Herman Heijermans' "Ghetto" premieres in Amsterdam
Foreign powers present the Chinese Empress with their list of 'irrevocable conditions' before their nations will withdraw troops from China
Herman Heijermans' "Hope of Blessing" premieres in Amsterdam
The French Chamber of Deputies and Senate pass a bill calling for an end to agitation or prosecutions against those involved in the Dreyfus affair, which has divided France since 1894
Private companies allowed to use the word "postcard" in the US. Previously they were labelled "Private Mailing Cards" and known as "souvenir cards".
German South West Africa abolishes the slavery of young children
Reginald Fessenden becoomes 1st to broadcast music over radio (disputed)
Luisa Tetrazzini sings to 250,000 people at Lotta's Fountain, San Francisco
Irving Fisher patents archiving system with index cards
German plane drops bombs on Dover, England

Ottawa’s Harry Broadbent scores in 10-0 Senators blowout of the Montreal Canadiens; first in 16-game NHL record goal-scoring streak; Charlie Simmer’s 13 game streak (1979) is considered modern-day record
BBC broadcasts first British radio play "The Truth about Father Christmas"
London Coloseum opens
Albania becomes a republic (ex-premier Ahmed Zogoe's coup)
First radio transmission of NCRV in the Netherlands
Richard Rodgers Theater (46th St Chanin's) opens at 226 W 46th NYC
School in Babb's Switch, Oklahoma, catches fire, 36 die
Test cricket debut of Walter Hammond, who scores 51 and takes 5-36 against South Africa
George Abbott Theater (Adelphi, 54 St) opens at 152 W 54th St NYC
F. García Lorca's "La zapatera prodigiosa" premieres in Madrid
Arturo Alessandri wins presidential election in Chile
National Council of Negro Women forms
First radioactive isotope medicine is administered in Berkeley, California
Dutch government recognizes Italian king Emanuel III as emperor of Abyssinia
First powered flight of V-1 'buzz bomb', Peenemunde, Germany
Red Army occupies German airfields at Tatsinskaya and Morozovsk
French Fourth Republic is founded
US General MacNarney gives 800,000 "minor Nazis" amnesty
First US completely solar heated house is occupied (Dover, Mass)
Greek government disbands due to state of war, press censorship
First opera commissioned for television - "Amahl and the Night Visitors" by Gian Carlo Menotti premieres on NBC with the NBC Opera Theater
The United Kingdom of Libya gains independence from Italy through the United Nations
KHOL (now KHGI) TV channel 13 in Kearney, NB (ABC) begins broadcasting
KOA (now KCNC) TV channel 4 in Denver, CO (NBC) begins broadcasting

The Wellington to Auckland night express train plunges into the Whangaehu River at Tangiwai after floods weaken the bridge, killing 151 of the 285 passengers and crew aboard in New Zealand’s worst railway accident
Two fast express trains crash head-on killing 103 in Czechoslovakia
Council for the Protection of Children forms in Netherlands
Laos gains its independence from France
"I Love Lucy" Christmas show airs, never put in syndication
Ferdinand de Lesseps statue blown up in Port Said Egypt
Dutch bishops question papacy values
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
Greeks and Turks riot in Cyprus
Soviet spacecraft Luna 13 lands on Moon
USAF C144 military charter aircraft crashes near Binh Thai, Vietnam, killing 129
China performs nuclear test at Lop Nor, PRC
Pirate Radio Pegasus starts broadcasting off New Zealand
Apollo 8 astronauts read passages from Book of Genesis

Ballon d'Or: Manchester United's winger George Best wins award for best European football player ahead of teammate Bobby Charlton and Red Star Belgrade winger Dragan Džajić; first Northern Irish national to win the award
WATU (now WAGT) TV channel 26 in Augusta, GA (CBS) begins broadcasting
9 Jews are convicted in Leningrad of hijacking a plane
Walt Disney releases animated musical "The Aristocats" featuring voices of Phil Harris, Eva Gabor, Hermione Baddeley, Sterling Holloway, and Scatman Crothers
District of Columbia Home Rule Act is passed, allowing residents of Washington, D.C. to elect their own local government
Ferryboat capsized off coast of Equador, drowning 200
"Ghost to the Post", Raiders tight end Dave Casper ("The Ghost") catches a 42-yard reception to set up the Raiders' tying field goal near the end of regulation (Raiders win in OT)
1st Ariane rocket launched
Americans remember hostages held by Iran by shining lights for 417 seconds
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Chaminade, with a student body of only 850 students, defeats #1 ranked Virginia 77-72 in a Honolulu holiday basketball classic
Palace coup in Mauritania
French hostage Aurel Cornea, held in Lebanon for 9 months, released
Iran offensive against Iraqi islands of Shatt al-Arab
Ballon d'Or: Marseille forward Jean-Pierre Papin is named Europe's best football player; first from the French League to win the trophy
Four Muslim fundamentalists capture Air France pilot in Algiers
Ballon d'Or: PSG/Milan's Liberian striker George Weah is named Europe's best football player; first edition where players born outside Europe permitted to receive votes
Sid El-Antri massacre (or Sidi Lamri) in Algeria kills 50 to 100 people
The Dominican Republic becomes a member of the Berne Convention copyright treaty
The Texas 7 hold up a sports store in Irving, Texas. Police officer Aubrey Hawkins is shot during the robbery
Spanish police thwart an attempt by ETA to detonate 50 kg of explosives at 3:55 p.m. inside Madrid's busy Chamartín Station.
Gary Siniseconverts to Catholicism, following his wife who converted in 2000
11 kindergarten children are killed after a minivan plunges into a roadside pond in Jiangxi, China
Beijing breaks its 1951 record for sub-zero December temperatures, recording more than 300 hours as a record cold spell affects the north and northeast of China
NASA's Parker Solar probe makes a record-breaking approach to the Sun, coming within 3.8 million miles (6 million kilometers) of the star
Thomas Wolsey appointed Lord Chancellor of England byHenry VIII
James Cookis first European to visit Kiritimati, also called Christmas Island
Jacobin plot againstNapoleonuncovered
Henry Fordcompletes his first useful petrol / gasoline fuelled engine
Enrico Carusogives his last public performance, with the Metropolitan Opera in NYC
Sukarnosentenced to four years in prison by Indonesian authorities in Bandung, Dutch East Indies
PopePius XIImakes a Christmas Eve appeal for peace during World War II
Terence Rattigan's comedy play "While the Sun Shines" premieres in London
US PresidentFDRappoints GeneralEisenhoweras Supreme Commander of the Allied Forces
AuthorAldous Huxleytakes LSD for the first time
AFL Championship, Balboa Stadium, San Diego: Houston Oilers beat San Diego Chargers 10-3;Billy Cannonscores game's only TD
Curt Floodwrites to baseball CommissionerBowie Kuhnrefusing to accept the Cardinals’ right to deal him and in favor of free agency
Peruvian Airlines Electra crashes at headwaters of Amazon, killing all exceptJuliane Margaret Koepckefound 10 days later
Former US astronautJohn Glennjoins the US Senate representing Ohio
The Sporting News bestows St Louis Cardinals outfielderLou Brocktheir MLB Player of the Year Award
Ballon d'Or: Juventus' French midfielderMichel Platiniis named Europe's best football player for the 3rd consecutive time; beats Verona striker Preben Elkjær and Barcelona midfielder Bernd Schuster
Charles Taylorstarts First Liberian Civil War when he enters country at the head of guerilla group the National Patriotic Front of Liberia, to overthrow President Samuel Doe
Panama's dictator,Manuel Noriega, seeks asylum in the Vatican embassy
Expos tradeTim Rainesto White Sox for Ivan Calderon & Barry Jones
Saddam Husseinsays Israel is Iraq's first target
US PresidentGeorge H. W. Bushpardons former National Security AdvisorRobert McFarlaneand former Secretary of Defense Caspar Weinberger for their roles in the Iran-contra affair
Ballon d'Or: Borussia Dortmund's German sweeper Matthias Sammer is named best football player in Europe ahead of PSV/Barcelona strikerRonaldoand Newcastle United strikerAlan Shearer
Opening of St Peter's Holy Door by PopeJohn Paul IIin approach of 3rd millennium
Pope Francisopens the Holy Door of St Peter's Basilica in Rome, inaugurating the church's Jubilee Year of Hope
Byzantine church Hagia Sophia in Constantinople is dedicated for the second time after being destroyed by earthquakes
Treaty of Ghent signed, ending the War of 1812 between the United States, the United Kingdom and their allies
Thomas Edisonfiles for a patent for the cylinder phonograph
First ships of admiral Nagumo's Pearl Harbor fleet return to Japan
"Time Magazine" names New York MayorRudy Giuliani'Person of the Year', for leadership after 9/11 attacks
